summary:
The agent concludes that while “major depression” may not be explicitly listed, it can still be identified in population health data due to systematic classification under maternal condition hierarchies encompassing psychological/emotional issues linked to poor prenatal outcomes. This reflects real-world implementation where mental health concerns are integrated into existing diagnostic structures used across maternity healthcare systems.

We are asked only to summarize the *agent’s reasoning*, keeping it exactly two sentences focused solely on key clinical points:

The agent reasons that although "major depression" is not explicitly mentioned in recorded maternal complication lists, it falls under recognized hierarchical classifications of maternal conditions tied to psychological distress during pregnancy. Because major depression strongly influences maternal-fetal outcomes and is routinely screened in antenatal care, it is reliably captured in population health databases through standard diagnostic coding systems—even when indirect.
